description_long: This is a rustic wooden cabin complex consisting of two main structures connected by an elevated covered walkway. The buildings feature steep, gabled roofs clad in weathered blue-green shingles, with visible wooden support beams and stilts elevating the structures above the ground. The exterior walls are constructed from dark, aged timber planks, giving the cabin a traditional, hand-built appearance. Several porches and stairways wrap around the buildings, providing access and architectural interest, while small windows are set into the gables and walls. The overall design suggests a lakeside or mountain retreat, with practical features for elevated terrain or flood-prone areas. The cabin's layout and multiple entry points make it suitable for communal or family use in a scenic, natural setting.
description: A rustic, elevated wooden cabin complex with steep gabled roofs, connected by a covered walkway and featuring porches, stairs, and small windows.
description_view_0: This view shows the two main cabin structures connected by a central elevated walkway, with porches and stairs visible on the left building.
description_view_1: From this end-on perspective, the gabled roofs and the depth of the buildings are prominent, with the walkway and porch railings visible in the background.
description_view_2: This side view highlights the symmetry of the two cabins, the connecting walkway, and the overhanging roofs, with the right building's porch more visible.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the front of the left cabin, showing the main entrance, porch, and the walkway leading to the rear building.
attribute_roof_type: steep gable roof with overhangs, covered in weathered asphalt shingles, featuring intersecting gables and dormer-like extensions
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple small rectangular windows, asymmetrically distributed on the gable ends and side walls, with some grouped in pairs and others set singly
attribute_door_placement: one main door centered on the front porch of the left building, with possible secondary entrances on the side porches and walkways
